channel.title: Diversity on Nature Works
channel.description: Recent content in Diversity on Nature Works

item.title: Nature Rising From the Rubble
item.description:
Sally Bower’s 2021 RHS Bursary report on growing in rubble substrate

Landscape designer Sally Bower has a very lovely study tour report about growing in low nutrient substrates. http://localhost:1313/blog/230913-nature-rising-from-the-rubble/

item.title: Low fertility recycled substrates
item.description:
Many recycled aggregates are low fertility and sterile, meaning they’re an ideal growing substrate for many native plants and wild flowers

I’m a big fan of John Little’s work on creating wildlife habitat diversity using recycled building “waste”. http://localhost:1313/blog/230912-low-fertility-recycled-substrates-native-wild-flowers/

item.title: It’s Behind You!
item.description:
Forging local connections

During my more low-level tasks, in amongst the flower beds and on the cutting room floor, my mind will often race ahead on the complexities of the Nature & Climate Emergency. http://localhost:1313/blog/230122-behind-you/

item.title: Diversity
item.description:
Notes for those who want to take positive action to improve the diversity of people involved with forest gardens and horticulture

87% of the UK identify as White. In the UK horticulture industry, the figure is 99%*. http://localhost:1313/diversity/
